Title: ClZISO mutation leads to photosensitive flesh in watermelon

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: ζ-carotene-derived downstream carotenoids including lycopene and β-carotene; ABA
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: flesh color/nutritional quality; ABA supports fruit development and ripening
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Central desaturation/isomerization segment upstream of lycopene formation
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: ZISO carotenoid isomerase; integral membrane isomerase
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Ubiquitously expressed in roots, stems, leaves, flowers and fruits; increases in wild-type flesh during fruit development
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in roots, stems, leaves, flowers and fruits; transcript rises with fruit development in WT flesh but remains relatively stable in psf
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes cis–trans isomerization of the central 15–15' double bond, converting 9,15,9'-tri-cis-ζ-carotene to 9,9'-di-cis-ζ-carotene
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms an immediate precursor required for downstream carotenoid and ABA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step supported; mutation blocks pathway flux and causes upstream ζ-carotene accumulation with reduced downstream carotenoids
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Wild-type ClZISO promotes carotenoid pathway flux beyond ζ-carotene, enabling lycopene/β-carotene accumulation, red flesh coloration, and normal fruit ABA increase; loss of function causes yellow photosensitive flesh with ζ-carotene accumulation and reduced ABA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: EMS mutant identification; BSA-seq fine mapping; nonsense mutation detection; qRT-PCR; chromoplast subcellular localization; heterologous E. coli enzyme assay with pAC-ZETA and HPLC; carotenoid profiling; light compensation assays in vitro and in vivo; RNA-seq
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Intense sunlight caused rapid flesh photobleaching; long-term white LED light partially restored downstream carotenoid accumulation and pink coloration via photoisomerization
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter used for mCherry localization construct in watermelon protoplasts
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, NCBI SRA: PRJNA759595; BSA-seq/resequencing reported, accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Substantially reduced total carotenoids in fruit; blocked normal ABA increase during fruit development; delayed hypocotyl greening; lighter flower and callus coloration
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for breeding or engineering flesh-color diversity in watermelon; avoid loss-of-function alleles when stable downstream carotenoid accumulation and ABA-associated ripening traits are desired; light environment may partially modulate phenotype
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The EMS mutation is a G-to-A transition at Chr07:30263420 causing premature stop codon (W273*). Mutant protein ClZISOmu retains chromoplast localization but loses catalytic activity. Light can partially compensate by nonenzymatic photoisomerization.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ClZISO was identified as the causal gene for the psf mutant by BSA-seq and mutation analysis. 2. ClZISO encodes a chromoplast-localized 15-cis-ζ-carotene isomerase converting 9,15,9'-tri-cis-ζ-carotene to 9,9'-di-cis-ζ-carotene. 3. Loss of ClZISO function causes ζ-carotene accumulation, reduction of downstream carotenoids and ABA, and yellow photosensitive flesh. 4. Light partially bypasses the enzymatic block through photoisomerization in vitro and in vivo.
